response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their bodies react to peanut proteins which trigger severe allergic reactions when ingested or even when exposed to airborne particles  
this reaction can lead to anaphylaxis which is a life-threatening condition that requires immediate medical attention and is not compatible with the environment of underwater living  

Sec. 2  
living underwater would require a person to be in a controlled environment such as a submarine or underwater habitat where food sources and air quality are managed  
however the presence of peanuts or peanut products in such environments would pose a risk to those with allergies making it impractical or unsafe for them to reside there permanently
